This is the guide that relates to the Desktop version , Console version , and Mobile version . For the guide that relates to the Old-gen console version and Nintendo 3DS version , see Legacy:Guide:Class setups . For detailed armor recommendations at every stage of the game, see Guide:Armor progression . "Setup" redirects here. For the game mechanic, see Loadouts . Terraria has no formal player class or leveling system. However, weapons can be grouped into four distinct categories based on their

uped into four distinct categories based on their damage type — melee , ranged , magic , and summoner . Alongside weapons, several armor sets and accessories operate in tandem to these and potentially can only benefit specific damage types and/or playstyles. Each class has its strengths and weaknesses and has a wide variety of weapons to choose from. Melee The melee class sports the highest overall survivability [ a ] bonuses of any class across progression as well as decent crowd control, but m

progression as well as decent crowd control, but many melee weapons are limited in range. Although short-ranged melee weapons tend to deal more damage and are less of a challenge to use with the class' high defense, those that fire projectiles are favored especially later in progression, where the former weapons require specialized defensive builds to use effectively. While melee has the highest buffs to defense and is the only class to specialize in increasing the player's health regeneration ,

e in increasing the player's health regeneration , other damage types do not , in contrast, specialize in stats like boosting mobility or improving evasiveness in exchange for their lower overall survivability (with very few exceptions in the lategame). Instead, they compensate in the form of direct damage increases. This leads to "tank" builds that can utilize melee armor pieces, which sacrifice the class's respective damage in favor of survivability bonuses, being viable in the late game. Rang

ility bonuses, being viable in the late game. Ranged Rangers are able to attack from a distance, doing high single-target, piercing, or splash damage. They most often utilize weapons like bows and guns , although more unusual forms of damage (referred to as "specialist" weapons) such as throwing weapons , weapons that can use darts , or others can also be relevant at specific points in progression. Ranged users must constantly acquire ammunition , unless using the Endless Quiver or Endless Muske

, unless using the Endless Quiver or Endless Musket Pouch or using a ranged weapon that doesn't require ammunition such as the Toxikarp or Paintball Gun . Magic Mages are fragile but highly varied in attack style, often utilizing special mechanics such as homing, lifesteal, and area-of-effect damage. They are held back by reliance on mana , which regenerates slowly unless the Mana Regeneration buff is in effect. Magic users can often sustain their mana using Mana Stars when fighting many enemies

r mana using Mana Stars when fighting many enemies at once, but must use mana potions if they wish to recover mana instantly during boss fights. A vast majority of magic weapons are specialized for crowd control, with especially strong single-target weapons only being unlocked far later in progression. Summoner Summoners deal most of their damage using autonomous minions and/or sentries , and their attacks usually have excellent accuracy. However, most summoning armor sets have low defense in ex

, most summoning armor sets have low defense in exchange for their high damage potential. Alongside minions and sentries, summoners can deal active damage in the form of whips . These are primary weapons that focus summons onto specific targets and can increase their damage output. Whips cause minions to switch targets to whichever enemy was last struck, which can be undesirable when fighting bosses that spawn smaller enemies. In such cases, it is often ideal to manually set one's minions to tar

s often ideal to manually set one's minions to target the boss, and use any weapon except whips. While many players do challenges where only summon damage is allowed (whips, summons, and mounts ), the summoning class was balanced around the idea of the player also using the strongest normal weapon available to them in addition to minions and whips. [ 1 ] Summoners can gain synergistic benefits from hybridizing with other classes: Melee armor pieces can provide higher survivability when using mel

es can provide higher survivability when using melee armor pieces in hybrid sets. Certain melee accessories (the Feral Claws and its upgrades) as well as melee armor pieces improve the speed of whips, as well. Both ranged and magic weapons are useful for supplementing longer-range damage in situations when whips would be less optimal. For magic damage users, whips can be used while the player waits for their mana to regenerate. Each class gains more complex abilities as the game progresses, enab

ore complex abilities as the game progresses, enabling more strategic variety in combat. It is advised for players to try out every weapon they find to help determine what works best for them. Although not required for anything, it is strongly encouraged to purchase a DPS Meter which can be bought from the Traveling Merchant , and to test your weapons on either Target Dummies or easy bosses that you have already killed and can summon multiple times (some weapons, like minions , have issues when

es (some weapons, like minions , have issues when the player attacks Target Dummies). Always be curious and, whenever possible, try to seek out new weapons or synergies with accessories that you think might be better than your current loadout; never strictly lock yourself to one type of class damage for an entire playthrough (unless you are doing a challenge). This also helps to gauge your progress throughout the entire playthrough. Furthermore, it is also encouraged to use buff potions and heal

it is also encouraged to use buff potions and healing potions as they broadly increase efficiency and survivability in combat (especially during events and boss fights) with little to no downside. In general, it is recommended to focus on gear that benefits one class. This is because equipment slots are limited, and many armors and accessories only benefit a specific class, especially later into the game. Summoners are an exception to this rule, since summons can deal damage alongside melee/rang

since summons can deal damage alongside melee/ranged/magic weapons, so summoning gear will always provide a benefit as long as summons are being used. All classes can use at least two minions and two sentries at all times even without using summoning gear or potions (by using a Bewitching Table or War Table ), so it's recommended to always utilize these free slots regardless of primary class for what is essentially free damage. [ b ] Using minion slot-boosting accessories can even be viable for

slot-boosting accessories can even be viable for non-summoners, if the player relies on percentage-based damage increases that apply to all damage classes, or is using mixed armor sets that both benefit their respective type of damage and summon damage. Note that although it is beneficial to generally focus on one class between melee/ranged/magic at a time, there is no real benefit to specializing in exclusively one class over the course of an entire playthrough, and one should strongly conside

ntire playthrough, and one should strongly consider switching classes if they happen to obtain a powerful weapon from a different class. Off-class weapons can and will be useful in situations where one's main class weapons are ineffective. Some examples of this would be using whips against enemies that can pass through walls where bullets can't reach, using swords against enemies where long-range damage is not as effective, or using things like bows, guns, or magic weapons against enemies that c

ows, guns, or magic weapons against enemies that can't be reached by swords. Compared to a traditional role-playing game, Terraria's classes are not set in stone, and the game allows for a large degree of freedom in which it is not nearly as punishing if off-class weapons are used. exclusive Hive Pack Expert-exclusive Mounts Golf Cart Chillet Roller Skates Buffs/Potions Food buffs Regeneration Potion Ironskin Potion Swiftness Potion Rage Potion [ d ] [ o ] Wrath Potion [ c ] Heartreach Potion Endurance Potion Gravitation Potion Summoning Potion Slice of Cake Pre- mechanical bosses At this point in the game, damage bonuses from armor and accessories become more significant, so it is encouraged at this point to choose a class to specialize in. Ranger is generally agreed to h

to specialize in. Ranger is generally agreed to have the best weapons at this stage. However, melee also improves significantly due to melee armors having significantly higher defense than any other class. Magic is still heavily focused on crowd control, making it an effective class for those who want to take out The Destroyer first. Summoners have their best weapons locked behind Dreadnautilus and Queen Slime , and as both bosses can present a challenge if the player is using a summoner loadou

challenge if the player is using a summoner loadout, it may be worth switching to a different loadout temporarily to deal with them. If either of these hurdles are passed, it is also recommended to mix your chosen class with summoner at this stage, if you are running a primarily offensive loadout. This is also the point where a fully-defensive build begins to diverge from damage builds thanks to the availability of the Flesh Knuckles and Star Veil coupled with greater access to damage accessorie

his point, players are expected to have chosen between melee, ranged, magic, summoning, or a hybrid of summoning and another class. This is also where significant armor variety starts to show up within the same class, with players choosing between Hallowed armor, Chlorophyte armor, and tier 2 Old One's Army armor. For magic and ranged, the choice lies between Hallowed armor (for offensive loadouts in need of a defensive effect) or Chlorophyte armor (for all-out offense). Melee users should use T

or (for all-out offense). Melee users should use Turtle armor for pure defense, or some combination of Hallowed/Chlorophyte Mask, Monk's Shirt/Squire's Plating, and Squire's Greaves for the highest overall stats. If a reliable source of Defender Medals is not available, full Chlorophyte armor also works for offensive melee sets, and for defensive ranged/magic sets. As was the case in previous stages, ranged provides the highest long-distance DPS , melee provides the best tanking ability, magic p

, melee provides the best tanking ability, magic provides the most unique weapon effects, and summoner provides the most effortless mob clearing. Biomes are the different types of areas that any Terraria world can contain. Every biome has its own characteristic terrain blocks , walls , collectible items , backgrounds , enemies , critters , theme music , Angler quest fish , and other features. Spanning the world's ceiling is the Space biome. Biomes on the Surface layer include Forests , Deserts , the Snow biome , the Jungle , the Dungeon , the evil biome (either the Corruption or the Crimson ), and at the far ends of the world, the two Oce

n ), and at the far ends of the world, the two Oceans . These Surface biomes extend into the Underground layer. Going any deeper, the player will find unique subterranean variants of most of these. These include the Underground Desert , Ice biome , and Underground Jungle . The Glowing Mushroom biome also appears in the Cavern layer, and the Underworld spans the entire bottom of the world. Once Hardmode is activated, a few new biomes form. The world's evil biome roots deeper with the Underground

ld's evil biome roots deeper with the Underground Corruption / Crimson , and the Hallow and Underground Hallow form as well. The Corruption, Crimson, and Hallow are all "contagious" biomes which spread to certain adjacent tiles. In pre-Hardmode , these biomes will only overtake other biomes via grasses , but once Hardmode begins all three biomes can spread directly through most soil blocks, vines , and thorny bushes . Relative to the world's horizontal center, the Snow biome and the Dungeon are

zontal center, the Snow biome and the Dungeon are always located on the same side, and the Jungle and the Underground Desert on the opposite side. [ 1 ] Space Space is the layer that appears at the top of the world. The sky is dark and the stars are visible, regardless of if the sun is up or not. Gravity is decreased in Space. Harpies spawn here from the start of the game, and Wyverns and Martian Probes during Hardmode. Surface and Underground Below Space are the Surface and Underground layers,

low Space are the Surface and Underground layers, separated by the 0 feet line. Biomes usually span both of these layers, and their actual "underground" versions do not begin until the depths of the Cavern layer. Forest Forests are the most common surface biomes, and are where the player usually spawns upon starting a new world. Green grass grows across the dirt , which can spawn Mushrooms , Dayblooms , and trees . Forests appear whenever no other biome is valid. Snow biome The Snow biome is a w

r biome is valid. Snow biome The Snow biome is a winter-themed biome predominately made of Snow and Ice Blocks . Snow falls constantly, though this is only an aesthetic effect. Relative to the world's horizontal center, the Snow biome is always located on the same side as the Dungeon and on the opposite side of the Jungle . [ 1 ] Desert Deserts are large expanses of Sand . Their vegetation is comparatively sparse, consisting of Cacti and Waterleaf . Corruption and Crimson A world will always gen

f . Corruption and Crimson A world will always generate with an evil biome – either Crimson or Corruption , but never both (unless in drunk worlds ). There is a 1/2 (50%) chance of either of the evil biomes generating, although it can be selected manually upon world creation. The evil biomes are hazardous biomes with mostly analogous contents: The Corruption follows a rotten theme and features vertical chasms, Shadow Orbs , and Demon Altars . The Crimson follows a gory theme and features angled

Crimson follows a gory theme and features angled passages, Crimson Hearts , and Crimson Altars . Jungle The Jungle is composed of Jungle grass , vines , Mud , Bamboo and Mahogany trees . The sky appears a vibrant green color. Mahogany trees regrow automatically without the need to plant Acorns . Relative to the horizontal center of the world, the Jungle is always located on the opposite side of the Snow biome and the Dungeon , [ 1 ] and the same side as the Underground Desert. Dungeon The Dunge

side as the Underground Desert. Dungeon The Dungeon is a labyrinth leading from the Surface down to near-Underworld depths. The Old Man stands at the entrance, and using him to defeat Skeletron is required to gain access to the inside. Heading below 0 feet in the Dungeon without defeating Skeletron spawns one or more Dungeon Guardians , which have enormous defense and are capable of one-hitting even endgame players. The loot found in the Dungeon is rare, valuable, and much of it cannot be found

is rare, valuable, and much of it cannot be found elsewhere. Relative to the world's horizontal center, the Dungeon is always located on the same side as the Snow biome and on the opposite side of the Jungle. [ 1 ] Ocean Oceans consist of a sandy shore against a deep body of water where dangerous swimming enemies are found as well as Water Chests which contain ocean-themed loot. They are always located on both horizontal edges of the world. Duke Fishron can be summoned here with a Truffle Worm

Fishron can be summoned here with a Truffle Worm . The Magic Conch can be used to teleport the player to an Ocean. Glowing Mushroom biome The surface version of the Glowing Mushroom biome does not occur naturally, and can only be cultivated by players using Mushroom Grass Seeds or an imported Clentaminator with Dark Blue Solution on surface-level Mud Blocks . Cavern The Cavern layer marks the beginning of distinct underground biome variants, with separate characteristics from their surface vers

h separate characteristics from their surface versions. Ice biome The Ice biome is the Cavern layer's more dangerous portion of the Snow biome and is located beneath the surface part. Many ledges and bodies of water will have a layer of Thin Ice over them, and while these can support the player's weight they can break when landing on them with high enough speed. Frozen Chests contain ice-themed equipment. Underground Desert The Underground Desert is located beneath the surface Desert. The biome

is located beneath the surface Desert. The biome always has either a circular or an oval shape and contains various types of Antlions as well as Hardmode enemies themed after ancient Egypt. Underground Jungle The Underground Jungle is located beneath the surface Jungle. Its dense catacomb of compartments contain several hazards and dense vegetation. In Hardmode, the valuable Chlorophyte Ore can be mined here. Three bosses can be fought in this biome: An Underground Jungle always contains severa

iome: An Underground Jungle always contains several Bee Hives , where the Queen Bee boss is usually summoned. After the mechanical bosses have been defeated, Plantera's Bulbs spawn, allowing the player to summon Plantera . The Jungle Temple is also located within the Underground Jungle, where the Golem boss can be summoned after Plantera is defeated. Underground Mushroom Underground Glowing Mushroom biomes feature Glowing Mushrooms on top of Mud Blocks , and if there is enough height in the cave

Blocks , and if there is enough height in the cavern, Giant Glowing Mushrooms can grow as well. They are the only place where Truffle Worms can be obtained. Underworld The Underworld spans the entire bottom of the world. Lava pools cover much of its terrain and dangerous enemies spawn frequently. Hellstone can be mined here, Ruined Houses offer valuable loot (some within their Shadow Chests ), and unique furniture, and the Wall of Flesh boss can be summoned here in order to activate Hardmode . I

be summoned here in order to activate Hardmode . In Hardmode, the Tortured Soul spawns in the Underworld, which can be converted to the Tax Collector NPC using Purification Powder . Hardmode Hardmode biomes spawn or convert as a result of the game transitioning to Hardmode . The Hallow The Hallow can be considered the counterpart to the evil biome . It is automatically created once the player activates Hardmode by defeating the Wall of Flesh , and has pastel-colored fairy-tale imagery. Undergrou

d has pastel-colored fairy-tale imagery. Underground Hallow The Underground Hallow is located beneath the surface Hallow, starting at the Cavern layer. It has a different soundtrack from the surface Hallow, and contains different enemies as well as valuable items, such as Souls of Light and Crystal Shards . Underground Corruption The Underground Corruption is created in the Cavern layer upon defeating the Wall of Flesh . It is not necessarily located beneath pre-Hardmode surface Corruption biome

ated beneath pre-Hardmode surface Corruption biomes. While it shares the theme with the surface Corruption, it has different enemies and a different soundtrack (a mix of the regular Underground and Corruption soundtrack). It is the only place where enemies drop Cursed Flames . It and the Underground Crimson are the only places to obtain Souls of Night , which are occasionally dropped by all enemies. Underground Crimson The Underground Crimson is the alternative to the Underground Corruption, whi

the alternative to the Underground Corruption, which functions and generates in mostly the same way. The Underground Crimson, like the Corruption variant, will have enemies occasionally drop Souls of Night . It is the only place where enemies drop Ichor . Corrupted, Crimson, and Hallowed Desert A world might initially generate with several Corrupted / Crimson Deserts . If the Hardmode conversion overlaps with a Desert, additional Corrupted/Crimson/ Hallowed Desert will be created. These spawn un

n/ Hallowed Desert will be created. These spawn unique versions of the Mummy and Sand Shark with unique drops. Sand is replaced by Ebonsand , Crimsand , or Pearlsand . Corrupted, Crimson, and Hallowed Ice When the world enters Hardmode, the Corruption , Crimson , and Hallow can spread through the Ice biome , generating Purple , Red , and Pink Ice Blocks , respectively. Pigrons of the respective color start to spawn in these areas. Mini-biomes Biomes in this category tend to be small in size, but

mes in this category tend to be small in size, but some make up for it with large numbers. They generally have unique characteristics and enemy or critter spawns, but usually have neither unique backgrounds nor music. Oasis The Oasis is a mini-biome that spawns in the Desert . It consists of palm trees surrounding a pool of water where cattail plants grow, attracting Dragonflies . If the player places enough water in the Desert, it will become an Oasis. Granite Cave Granite Caves are cave areas

Oasis. Granite Cave Granite Caves are cave areas made completely of Granite Blocks , usually fairly spacious with interspersed ledges and platforms. They contain granite-themed enemies such as Granite Elementals and Granite Golems . Marble Cave Marble Caves are cave areas that are horizontal in shape and made completely out of Marble Blocks . They contain ancient-Greek-themed enemies such as Hoplites and Medusas . Spider Nest Spider Nests are characterized by black background walls and numerous

aracterized by black background walls and numerous webs filling the area. This mini-biome will spawn Wall Creepers in pre-Hardmode and Black Recluses during Hardmode. The Cobwebs rapidly grow back, which is a unique feature of the biome. Spider Nests are the only location where Web Covered Chests are found, and therefore the only place to obtain a Web Slinger . The Stylist NPC is found in this mini-biome. Bee Hive Bee Hives are located in the Underground Jungle . They are made from Hive blocks a

erground Jungle . They are made from Hive blocks and contain honey and often a Larva . Destroying the Larva summons the Queen Bee boss. Breaking a Hive block may spawn a Bee and/or some honey. Glowing moss biome Glowing moss biomes are located in the Caverns layer of the world, but never under the spawn area. Within them, almost every exposed Stone Block is covered in glowing moss . The glowing moss can be any variant other than Helium Moss. Every instance of the biome in a single world will use

y instance of the biome in a single world will use the same moss variant. This mini-biome is much larger than the Moss chamber . There can be up to 2, 3, or 4 instances of this biome, depending on world size , though there is no guarantee that any are generated. There are no unique enemies in glowing moss biomes. Ash forest Ash forests are barren, fiery-looking forests located on the edges of the Underworld . They feature Ash trees growing on Ash grass . There are always exactly 2 ash forests, w

grass . There are always exactly 2 ash forests, which fully occupy the leftmost and rightmost sixths of the Underworld. They are the only significant vegetation in the Underworld. There are no unique enemies in ash forests. Jungle Temple The Jungle Temple is a large, nearly impenetrable structure that spawns deep in the Underground Jungle. It is filled with powerful enemies and is generally only accessible after defeating Plantera , using the Temple Key she drops to open the door. In the larges

mple Key she drops to open the door. In the largest chamber, there is a Lihzahrd Altar to spawn Golem with a Lihzahrd Power Cell . Meteorite The Meteorite biome is sometimes generated after the player defeats the Eater of Worlds or Brain of Cthulhu . There is a 1/2 (50%) chance that within the next day at midnight the game will spawn a Meteorite, converting an area of the Surface into a crater with three layers. Blocks in the innermost area will be deleted without dropping anything; surrounding

be deleted without dropping anything; surrounding this, all blocks around the empty area will be converted into Meteorite ore; and in a much larger radius outside this mass of ore, only occasional blocks similarly converted. Meteorite ore will afflict the player with the Burning debuff on contact, unless they either have an accessory to nullify its effects, or are under the effect of an Obsidian Skin Potion . Mining Meteorite requires 50% pickaxe power , i.e. a Tungsten Pickaxe , Gold Pickaxe ,

power , i.e. a Tungsten Pickaxe , Gold Pickaxe , or better is mandatory. Meteor Heads override all native enemies of the biome the meteor crashed into. Town A Town is a player-created mini-biome that can be made by having three or more NPCs in close proximity. In Classic mode, they negate enemy spawn rates entirely; even in Expert and Master mode they will sharply reduce enemy spawns. However, enemy spawns are not affected when an event is occurring. Graveyard Graveyards appear when there are a

ring. Graveyard Graveyards appear when there are at least 7 Tombstones close to each other. They generate Ecto Mist and spawn enemies such as Ravens , Ghosts , Maggot Zombies , Moss Zombie , and Statue as well as spawn the typical nighttime enemies, even during the day. The Graveyard can only be manually created by the player, and will never generate naturally. Aether The Aether is a mini-biome that generates in the outermost edges of the world in either the Underground or Cavern layers. Only a

n either the Underground or Cavern layers. Only a single Aether biome can be generated per world, always being located on the same side as the Jungle . The main feature of this biome is a large pool of Shimmer found in its center, which is surrounded by various Gem Trees . Faelings can sometimes be found flying around the center pool. Micro-biomes These biomes have unique tiles or structures, but are very small and have no other exclusive properties. Flower patch Flower patches are small patches

ies. Flower patch Flower patches are small patches of Surface ground densely populated by flowers. Stone patch Stone patches are small patches of ground made of stone found at the surface, consisting of Stone Blocks with matching ambient objects on the top. Large ore vein Large ore veins are located at the surface layer and consist of a large vein of Copper / Tin or Iron / Lead Ore buried near the ground, with a few ore blocks above it. Moss chamber Moss chambers are very small chambers located

ber Moss chambers are very small chambers located in the Underground and Cavern layers that contain one color of moss with a matching back wall . Gemstone cave Gemstone caves are filled with concentrated gem deposits of one or more varieties and feature a unique background ( Gemstone Walls ) that is speckled with gems of the same color found in the gemstone cave. Thin Ice patch Thin Ice patches are micro-biomes found in the Ice biome that are made entirely of Thin Ice and occasional ore veins. S

e entirely of Thin Ice and occasional ore veins. Spike Caves Spike Caves are pits filled with Spikes that generate outside the Dungeon in worlds that have the No Traps and For the Worthy seeds without the Celebration Mk 10 seed. Treasure rooms Treasure rooms are spawned upon world creation and appear as if they were man-made structures. They generally contain loot and furniture . Underground Cabin Underground Cabins are found in the Cavern layer. They are typically wooden buildings full of dusty

They are typically wooden buildings full of dusty, broken furniture as well as Chests, pots, and occasionally usable furniture and decorations. Underground Cabins spawn with blocks representing their biome. If the player patches the holes in the walls and fulfills all NPC requirements, NPCs can move in. Living Tree Living Trees are huge, thick trees consisting of foreground blocks (as opposed to regular background trees ). Cutting the tree yields regular Wood , while the leaves yield nothing. M

s regular Wood , while the leaves yield nothing. Most Living Trees are hollow and lead deep underground. There may be rooms containing Living Wood Chests with related loot inside, as well as Living Wood furniture . It is not guaranteed that one will spawn in every world. Gnomes will spawn more often in front of Living Wood Walls . Floating Island Floating Islands are independent masses of land in the sky. They contain a Skyware Chest with valuable loot like the Starfury , the Shiny Red Balloon ,

e loot like the Starfury , the Shiny Red Balloon , the Lucky Horseshoe , or the Fledgling Wings . Deposits of ore can be found sometimes as well. Depending on world size, there are between two and nine Floating Islands in a world. Floating Lakes are a variation of Floating Islands and do not contain a Skyware Chest, but instead contain a lake for fishing in. Living Mahogany Tree Living Mahogany Trees are large trees generated in the Underground Jungle. Cutting the tree yields Rich Mahogany wood,

ungle. Cutting the tree yields Rich Mahogany wood, while the leaves yield nothing. They always have a hollow trunk, which contains an Ivy Chest. Jungle Shrine Jungle Shrines are small brick buildings and typically contain only a single Ivy Chest and a light source . They are found in the Underground Jungle and several can appear in one biome. Ruined House Ruined Houses are generated upon world creation in The Underworld in the middle 50% of the Underworld. They are towers with multiple levels, c

nderworld. They are towers with multiple levels, constructed with missing parts for a "ruined" look, from Obsidian Brick or Hellstone Brick . They are sometimes partially submerged in lava and there are often deposits of Hellstone just outside their walls. Pyramid Pyramids are rarely generated in the Desert upon world creation. They are made of Sandstone Bricks and contain a narrow passage. The treasure room is filled with Coin Stashes, pots and a Gold Chest that can contain unique loot. There i

a Gold Chest that can contain unique loot. There is also a passage to a cave at the bottom. Enchanted Sword Shrine Enchanted Sword Shrines house a breakable background sword sprite, which has a 1/30 (3.33%) chance of dropping a Terragrim , and a 29/30 (96.67%) chance of dropping an Enchanted Sword when destroyed. Mosaic Mosaics are found in the Underground and Cavern layers, consisting of a small room with Gold Coin Piles and tiles randomly set inside. Campsite Campsites are found in the Undergr

nside. Campsite Campsites are found in the Underground and Cavern layers, usually containing a Campfire , a few Silver /Gold Coin Piles, and an ambient object of skeletal remains. The campsite is 12–20 blocks wide, and not quite as tall. Other biomes This biome does not fit into any of the above categories. Abandoned Minecart Track Abandoned Minecart Tracks are found in the Underground and Cavern layers. They are long, continuous paths of Minecart Tracks which always have enough space for the pl

t Tracks which always have enough space for the player to ride to the end unhindered and often connect multiple larger cave systems. The length and number of Abandoned Minecart Tracks in a world are both proportional to its size, see World size for a detailed comparison. Some Abandoned Minecart Tracks generate extra-long, roughly 3 times longer than standard tracks and can span across multiple biomes. Note that the journey is not guaranteed to be safe. While riding, the player is likely to bump

safe. While riding, the player is likely to bump into enemies, and the tracks may also lead through liquids including lava or be rigged with Pressure Plate Track Traps . Removed biomes The following two biomes were exclusive to the Mobile version , and were removed in the Mobile 1.3.0.7 update. Heart Shrine Heart Shrines were heart-shaped caves sometimes found in Snow biomes. They held two Heart Crystals and a Chest that contained heart-themed items for the Valentine's Day celebration event. Ju

tems for the Valentine's Day celebration event. Jungle Sanctum A Jungle Sanctum was a structure that occasionally appeared in the Jungle. Jungle Sanctums were larger versions of Jungle Shrines and contained Coin Stashes, along with an Ivy Chest that contained several Jungle-themed items. Hybrid biomes Although many basic biomes appear at the world generation or are triggered by events, hybrid biomes may be synthesized by world mechanics or through player action. Hybrid biomes are those created b

h player action. Hybrid biomes are those created by the requirements being met for multiple biomes in a certain area. The simplest examples are Hallowed, Crimson, and Corrupted Deserts; however, not only Deserts can be hybridized. Floating Islands can be Corrupted/Crimsoned or Hallowed upon entry of Hardmode, and such high-layered biomes are even required by some Angler quests. Player action can similarly introduce Corruption, Crimson, Hallow, Desert, Jungle, Mushroom, Meteorite, or Snow biomes

sert, Jungle, Mushroom, Meteorite, or Snow biomes to any location from Underworld to Space. This can lead to, for example in an Underworld Crimson, Underworld and Crimson enemies both spawning in the applicable areas. Biome existence requirements The area used for counting required tiles for the existence of biomes at a location is 84 tiles to each side and 61 below and 62 above, measured from the player's center [ 2 ] . This is the same range as for area buffs such as Campfires and Bast Statues

for area buffs such as Campfires and Bast Statues . All Notes For Hallowed and Crimson/Corruption biome detection: There is a Hallowed value, a Crimson value, and a Corruption value. Each Hallowed tile adds 1 to the Hallowed value and subtracts 1 from both the Crimson and Corruption values. Each Crimson and Corruption tile adds 1 to its resp

ach Crimson and Corruption tile adds 1 to its respective biome value and subtracts 1 from the Hallowed value. This can be proven by placing 125 Hallowed tiles in an otherwise completely neutral area, observing that the area will change to Hallowed on placement of the 125th tile, then that it will return to neutral on placement of a Crimson or Corruption tile, then back to Hallowed on placement of an additional Hallowed tile, ad infinitum. For Snow biome detection: While the area functionally cha

w biome detection: While the area functionally changes to a Snow biome at 1,500 related tiles, the background of a Snow biome will not appear until 1,501 Snow biome tiles are present. For Glowing Mushroom biome detection: While the area functionally changes to a Glowing Mushroom biome at 101 related tiles, the background of a Glowing Mushroom biome will not appear until 201 Glowing Mushroom biome tiles are present. The Funkytown achievement requires 200 Glowing Mushroom biome tiles. For Dungeon

res 200 Glowing Mushroom biome tiles. For Dungeon biome detection: The game checks the number of Dungeon tiles (≥250), and checks only the single background wall the player is standing in front of. Inactive tiles ( actuated tiles) will count towards the formation of a biome. Trivia There were originally supposed to be alternative biomes for the Jungle, Underworld, and Hallow called Swamp , Hell, and Candyland , respectively, which would have replaced the original biomes in the same way the Crims

aced the original biomes in the same way the Crimson occasionally replaces the Corruption. The Hell was going to be based on the Hell from the 1997 American animated musical fantasy movie Hercules . However, these ideas were scrapped as the developers changed direction. An example of an event. In this case, it is the Frost Legion . See also: Seasonal events Events (some also known as invasions ) are temporary occasions where varieties of special enemies spawn, most at elevated spawn rates, even near NPCs , and attack players. Some events can occur randomly during gameplay, while others can be triggered via certain summoning items or special circumstances. Events spawn their own unique enemies not seen during ordinary gameplay, which can, in turn, drop unique it

inary gameplay, which can, in turn, drop unique items that are only obtainable within the event. Some events also bring unique visuals and a unique atmosphere, such as an altered moon and noticeable shaders applied to the screen. Events also are accompanied by their own special music , adding on to their theme. Depending on the particular event, they may last until a certain time limit is reached, or until a certain number of enemies are defeated. A progress bar can be seen during certain events

. A progress bar can be seen during certain events, which indicates the player's progression in the event and how far they are from completing it. It only appears during these events: Goblin Army , Old One's Army , Frost Legion , Pirate Invasion , Pumpkin Moon , Frost Moon , and Martian Madness . Other time-limited events ( Blood Moon , Rain , Slime Rain , Sandstorm , Solar Eclipse , etc.) do not have a progress bar. See Notes for more details about the progress bar. Invasion-type events have lo

out the progress bar. Invasion-type events have location requirements. The player must be no deeper than 67.5 tiles below the surface and within 187.5 tiles horizontally of the location of the invasion to be considered in the range of it, otherwise no invasion enemies will spawn. If the invasion is near the horizontal world center, then the horizontal check can also be satisfied by the player being within 187.5 tiles horizontally of any town NPC, although at a reduced spawn rate. The town NPC's

although at a reduced spawn rate. The town NPC's vertical position is not checked and so they can be housed far above or below the arena. See Notes for more details about invasions. There are 19 different events. Peaceful events Party Initiated randomly by NPCs during the day, or by manually activating a Party Center . Party (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) During a Party , most NPCs , town pets, the Travelling Merchant , and even Bunnies can be seen wearing a Party Hat . NPCs will displa

can be seen wearing a Party Hat . NPCs will display unique dialog when spoken to and some will sell unique items. While a Party is going on, different colored balloons are floating all around the sky in the background. The event will always end on dusk . Lantern Night Occurs the night after a boss or invasion has been defeated for the first time, or randomly at night after the Moon Lord has been defeated. Lantern Night (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) The Lantern Night is an event that occ

e versions) The Lantern Night is an event that occurs in celebration of the player's victory over a boss or invasion. The Party Girl will sell the Release Lantern and the player will have a flat +0.3 increase to their Luck stat. During such a night many lanterns can be seen rising up into the sky in the background. Weather events Rain Occurs randomly. Rain Rain affects all surface biomes for its duration. Each Rain event can last anywhere from a few in-game hours to several in-game days, potenti

few in-game hours to several in-game days, potentially spanning both day and night portions. When a player is in a Snow biome during Rain, it appears as a Blizzard , causing snowfall volume to increase. Rain also increases cloud cover. In addition to regular surface enemies, the following enemies will spawn: Pre-Hardmode : Flying Fish Umbrella Slime Raincoat Zombie (night) During blizzards: Deerclops (rare) During Hardmode : Angry Nimbus (uncommon) Rainbow Slime ( The Hallow , rare) During Hardm

n) Rainbow Slime ( The Hallow , rare) During Hardmode blizzards: Ice Golem (rare) Other effects include the following: There is a +20% Fishing Power bonus in all liquid bodies during Rain. Waterleaf blooms. Worms spawn frequently in Forest biomes . NPCs remain in their houses , as if it were night . The player character will squint in blizzards if they are outside. Sandstorm Occurs randomly while Wind speeds are above 30 mph . Sandstorm (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) A Sandstorm is an ev

Console and Mobile versions) A Sandstorm is an event specific to the Desert , they will not occur anywhere else. A Sandstorm occurs in all Deserts at the same time. While this event is active, the Mighty Wind debuff will be continuously inflicted upon the player. During a Sandstorm, many particles of sand can be seen flying across the screen, the color of these particles will depend on whether the Desert is regular, Corrupt , Crimson , or Hallow . Vision is also limited. In addition to regular

. Vision is also limited. In addition to regular Desert enemies, the following enemies will spawn: Pre-Hardmode : Angry Tumbler Antlion Charger Antlion Swarmer Hardmode : Sand Elemental Dune Splicer Sand Shark (On normal desert) Crystal Thresher (On Hallowed Desert ) Bone Biter (On Corrupt Desert ) Flesh Reaver (On Crimson Desert ) Windy Day Occurs randomly. Windy Day (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) A Windy Day results in increased cloud cover and stronger wind effects. If a Windy Day ha

cover and stronger wind effects. If a Windy Day happens at the same time as a Rain, the result is a Thunderstorm. In addition to regular Forest surface enemies, the following enemies will spawn: Windy Balloon Angry Dandelion Thunderstorm Occurs randomly. Thunderstorm (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) A Thunderstorm is a rare weather event where both the Rain and Windy Day events combine. During a Thunderstorm, lightning will occasionally strike trees, tiles and furniture, dealing damage to

ike trees, tiles and furniture, dealing damage to both enemies and players. The wind also blows much more ferociously than normal, and the lighting is dimmer than during Rain. All effects from the Rain event also occur during a Thunderstorm. Starfall Occurs randomly at night. Starfall (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) A Starfall is an event during which falling stars appear all throughout the sky in the background. Starfalls greatly increase the spawn rate of Fallen Stars , and causes Encha

the spawn rate of Fallen Stars , and causes Enchanted Nightcrawlers to spawn, resulting in easily obtainable bait . Pre-Hardmode events Blood Moon Occurs with a 1/9 (11.11%) chance if at least one player in the world has more than 120 health and when it is not a new moon , or when manually summoned with a Bloody Tear . Blood Moon The Blood Moon is rising... A Blood Moon is an event that takes place over the course of an entire night , from 7:30 PM to 4:30 AM, affecting only the surface layer. B

PM to 4:30 AM, affecting only the surface layer. Blood Moons are signaled by the status message " The Blood Moon is rising... ". All the water in the world, even underground, will turn blood-red for the duration of the event, including rain and the moon itself. A red filter will be applied to the screen when the player is on the surface layer. The ability to speed up time by sleeping in a bed is also disabled. The event will significantly boost spawn rates in surface biomes, as well as spawn new

pawn rates in surface biomes, as well as spawn new and dangerous enemies, while also changing their behavior and spawning conditions. Fishing Power is increased by 1.1 times. However, fishing could also bring about unique enemies. In addition to regular surface enemies, the following enemies will spawn: Anytime: The Groom The Bride Blood Zombie Drippler On a Corruption world: Corrupt Bunny Corrupt Goldfish Corrupt Penguin On a Crimson world: Vicious Bunny Vicious Goldfish Vicious Penguin From Fi

ous Bunny Vicious Goldfish Vicious Penguin From Fishing : Wandering Eye Fish Zombie Merman During Hardmode : Clown Chattering Teeth Bomb From Fishing during Hardmode : Hemogoblin Shark Blood Eel Dreadnautilus Blood Squid Deathweed blooms during a Blood Moon. event, the following enemies will spawn: Pre-Hardmode : Goblin Peon Goblin Sorcerer Goblin Thief Goblin Warrior Goblin Archer During Hardmode : Goblin Warlock Shadowflame Apparition (from Goblin Warlock ) Although they do not appear in the Goblin Army, Goblin Scouts can be found rarely on the outer thirds of the map. ted after a certain number of goblins have been defeated. Afterward, the Goblin Tinkerer NPC can be found underground . Slime Rain Occurs when a player has over 140 health and 8 defense , except in Expert Mode , where it can occur with a significantly decreased chance before meeting the criteria. Slime Rain (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Slime is falling from the sky! Slime Rain is an event where different variations of slimes constantly fall from the sky. If the player kills 150 slimes

fall from the sky. If the player kills 150 slimes before the event ends naturally, King Slime will spawn. If King Slime was defeated previously, then only 75 slimes need to be killed before he spawns. Many types of slimes spawn, including: Blue Slime Green Slime Purple Slime Pinky King Slime After King Slime is defeated, the event will immediately terminate. Old One's Army Initiated by placing an Eternia Crystal on an Eternia Crystal Stand . Old One's Army (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions)

One's Army (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) The old one's army is approaching! The Old One's Army is a unique Dungeon Defenders 2 crossover event that can be initiated after rescuing the Tavernkeep NPC , who can be found on any layer after defeating the Eater of Worlds / Brain of Cthulhu . The objective is to protect the Eternia Crystal from waves of enemies which appear from Mysterious Portals , seeking to destroy it. If the Crystal is destroyed by enemies, the event terminates. If it is

troyed by enemies, the event terminates. If it is kept safe until all waves are cleared, the event is won. Both the Crystal and its Stand are purchased from the Tavernkeep for coins . Players are inflicted with the Creative Shock debuff for the duration of the event, which prevents the removal or placement of blocks during the entire event within a certain range. Each wave becomes progressively more difficult and features larger varieties of enemies. Additionally, the entire event is expanded in

ies. Additionally, the entire event is expanded in difficulty depending on player progression. If triggered before any Mechanical Bosses have been defeated, the event consists of 5 waves. If triggered after at least one Mechanical Boss is defeated, the event consists of 7 waves. Once Golem has been defeated, the event becomes even more difficult with the boss Betsy spawning in the final wave. Betsy will only spawn once during each event, and defeating her will complete the event. The event also

ating her will complete the event. The event also contains 2 mini-bosses; the Ogre and the Dark Mage . During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Structures: Mysterious Portal Pre- mechanical bosses : Etherian Goblin Etherian Goblin Bomber Etherian Javelin Thrower Etherian Wyvern Dark Mage Old One's Skeleton (from Dark Mage ) After defeating one mechanical boss : Kobold Drakin Kobold Glider Wither Beast Ogre Post- Golem : Etherian Lightning Bug Betsy The Torch God Initiated when around

ing Bug Betsy The Torch God Initiated when around 100 torches accumulate in close proximity anywhere below the surface layer. The Torch God (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) The Torch God is a mini-event that happens when 100 torches accumulate in close proximity underground. The placed torches begin launching small fireballs at the player, extinguishing themselves in the process. The fireballs deal 40 / 80 / 120 damage, and they travel in straight lines through blocks . They can also spawn

traight lines through blocks . They can also spawn from torches located off-screen. While the event is active, the player is inflicted with the Blackout debuff , significantly reducing the player's light vision. When all torches are extinguished, the event ends. After a brief period, the torches reignite, and if 100 or more torches have been activated, the Torch God's Favor will appear in the player's inventory . The player can not challenge the event again once the player has a Torch God's Favo

event again once the player has a Torch God's Favor either in their inventory or consumed, and must create another character to do so. A player that has not consumed a Torch God's Favor can continuously challenge and complete the event and obtain more Torch God's Favors, as long as they do not have one in their inventory. Hardmode events Frost Legion Summoned with a Snow Globe . Frost Legion The Frost Legion has arrived! The Frost Legion is an event that functions similarly to the Goblin Army ,

ent that functions similarly to the Goblin Army , but it requires an item to start the event, which is only attainable in Hardmode . During Christmas , enemies will drop presents . If the world is in Hardmode, these presents have a 1/13 (7.69%) chance of containing a Snow Globe , which is used to spawn the Frost Legion. During the event, three different types of unique snowmen will spawn: Mister Stabby Snowman Gangsta Snow Balla All of the snowmen drop Snow Blocks , technically granting the play

n drop Snow Blocks , technically granting the player a way to obtain infinite amounts of Snow. The event is completed after a certain number of snowmen have been defeated. Afterward, the Santa Claus NPC will spawn during Christmas . Solar Eclipse Occurs with a 1/20 (5%) chance each day upon dawn, as soon as any mechanical boss has been defeated, or when manually summoned with a Solar Tablet . Solar Eclipse A solar eclipse is happening! The Solar Eclipse is a Hardmode event where the sun will ris

Eclipse is a Hardmode event where the sun will rise, obscured by the moon, dramatically reducing natural light to a level darker than nighttime. Normal biome enemy spawns are replaced with special enemies that can spawn near NPCs, much more frequently and in greater numbers than usual. During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Anytime: Eyezor Frankenstein Swamp Thing Vampire Creature from the Deep Fritz The Possessed After defeating all mechanical bosses : Reaper After defeating Plante

mechanical bosses : Reaper After defeating Plantera : Baby Mothron Mothron Butcher Deadly Sphere Dr. Man Fly Nailhead Psycho Pirate Invasion Occurs randomly during the day if a player has over 200 health , or when manually summoned with a Pirate Map . Pirate Invasion The pirates have arrived! The Pirate Invasion is an event that is very similar to the Goblin Army . It can spawn randomly, or be summoned by the use of a Pirate Map , which can be obtained from killing any enemy in the Ocean biome

btained from killing any enemy in the Ocean biome during Hardmode . During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Parrot Pirate Captain Pirate Corsair Pirate Crossbower Pirate Deadeye Pirate Deckhand Pirate's Curse Flying Dutchman Pumpkin Moon Summoned at night with a Pumpkin Moon Medallion . Pumpkin Moon The Pumpkin Moon is rising... The Pumpkin Moon is a Halloween -themed event that lasts until the next dawn. Unlike most other events, the Pumpkin Moon takes place in distinct waves. Each

Pumpkin Moon takes place in distinct waves. Each wave is finished by getting enough points by defeating enemies, with higher waves having higher difficulty. If enough enemies are defeated, the event will eventually enter the final wave where event enemies spawn endlessly. During the event, the moon visually turns into a jack-o-lantern. During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Scarecrow Splinterling Hellhound Poltergeist Headless Horseman Mourning Wood (boss) Pumpking (boss) Frost Moo

man Mourning Wood (boss) Pumpking (boss) Frost Moon Summoned at night with a Naughty Present . Frost Moon The Frost Moon is rising... The Frost Moon is a Christmas -themed event that lasts until the next dawn and uses the same wave system as the Pumpkin Moon. Enemies have similar behavior to Pumpkin Moon counterparts, but are tougher. During the event, the moon visually turns into a snowman's face. During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Present Mimic Flocko Gingerbread Man Zombie El

wn: Present Mimic Flocko Gingerbread Man Zombie Elf Elf Archer Nutcracker Yeti Elf Copter Krampus Everscream (boss) Santa-NK1 (boss) Ice Queen (boss) Although there are no rewards for surviving the event, besides an achievement , the enemies can drop unique items. Martian Madness Initiated by letting a Martian Probe escape from view after detecting a player. Martian Madness (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Martians are invading! The Martian Madness event is a post- Golem event with an alie

Madness event is a post- Golem event with an alien invasion theme. This event is unique from the others in that in order to start it, it is required to encounter a Martian Probe randomly in the surface and space layers of the world and move into its light. Once the Martian Probe detects the player, it needs to leave the screen without being destroyed. During the event, UFOs can be seen flying in the background. During the event, the following enemies will spawn: Martian Saucer Scutlix Martian W

emies will spawn: Martian Saucer Scutlix Martian Walker Martian Drone Tesla Turret Gigazapper Martian Engineer Martian Officer Ray Gunner Gray Grunt Brain Scrambler Lunar Events Initiated after defeating the Lunatic Cultist . Lunar Events (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Celestial creatures are invading! The Lunar Events is a unique type of event that spawns four different pillars, the Celestial Pillars , spread evenly across the world. While in the proximity of a pillar, respectively-them

le in the proximity of a pillar, respectively-themed enemies spawn. There are four different pillars: the Solar Pillar , the Vortex Pillar , the Nebula Pillar , and the Stardust Pillar . Initially, all pillars are surrounded by a protective shield. In order to damage and eventually defeat a pillar, a certain number of enemies related to said pillar needs to be defeated. There are numerous unique enemies in this event: Characters In the Solar area: Solar Pillar (boss) Drakomire Rider Selenian Dra

: Solar Pillar (boss) Drakomire Rider Selenian Drakanian Corite Sroller Crawltipede Drakomire In the Vortex area: Vortex Pillar (boss) Vortexian Alien Queen Alien Hornet Alien Larva Storm Diver In the Nebula area: Nebula Pillar (boss) Nebula Floater Brain Suckler Predictor Evolution Beast In the Stardust area: Stardust Pillar (boss) Star Cell Mini Star Cell Flow Invader Twinkle Popper Twinkle Milkyway Weaver Stargazer After defeating all four pillars: Moon Lord (boss) Notes Some events are class

lars: Moon Lord (boss) Notes Some events are classified as "invasions" and have their respective "invasion ID" [ 1 ] internally. Only one invasion can happen at a time. When an invasion is active, attempts to start other invasions will fail. Invasions also have the following features: Most invasions start at left or right world border and take some time to move to the world spawn point . Invasions move at the speed of 1 tile per tick [ 2 ] , or 60 tiles per second (which is affected by time spee

0 tiles per second (which is affected by time speed, e.g. time slider in Journey Mode , Enchanted Sundial / Moondial , sleeping ). [ 3 ] Assuming the world spawn point is at the horizontal center of the world, invasions take 35, 53, and 70 seconds to reach spawn point in small, medium, and large world size , respectively. The status message informing the player about an incoming invasion (e.g. " A goblin army is approaching from the west! " for Goblin Army ) will be displayed once per minute bef

oblin Army ) will be displayed once per minute before the invasion reaches spawn point. As a result, the message will be displayed twice in large worlds. Unlike other invasions, the Martian Madness starts at world spawn point, so event enemies can immediately begin spawning after the invasion started. As said above, the player must be no deeper than 67.5 tiles below the surface and within 187.5 tiles horizontally of the location of the invasion to be considered in the range of it, otherwise no i

o be considered in the range of it, otherwise no invasion enemies will spawn. The horizontal check can also be satisfied by the player being within 187.5 tiles horizontally of any town NPC, although having reduced enemy spawn rate, if the invasion has reached no more than 5 tiles from the horizontal center of the world. The town NPC's vertical position is not checked. The player has to kill enough enemies to complete the invasion. For each existing player, the required kill count is increased by

ng player, the required kill count is increased by a fixed amount. The formulas are shown in the table below. [ 4 ] Invasion ID Required kill count (No invasion active) 0 Goblin Army 1 [math]\displaystyle{ 80+40\times\text{Player count} }[/math] Frost Legion 2 [math]\displaystyle{ 80+40\times\text{Player count} }[/math] Pirate Invasion 3 [math]\displaystyle{ 120+60\times\text{Player count} }[/math] Martian Madness (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 4 [math]\displaystyle{ 160+40\times\text{Pl

sions) 4 [math]\displaystyle{ 160+40\times\text{Player count} }[/math] The display mode for progress bar for events can be changed in settings , with 3 options available: On , Off , or Timed . On : Default option . The progress bar will be displayed if any event-related enemy is within a rectangular area centered on player with a size of about 384.5×384.5-tile. [ 5 ] For Pumpkin Moon , Frost Moon , or invasions, it also requires the player to be above surface layer. When the conditions are not m

above surface layer. When the conditions are not met, the progress bar will be hidden after 2.67 seconds. Off : The progress bar will not be displayed. Timed : The progress bar will be displayed for 2.67 seconds after killing an event-related enemy, or the event progresses to the next wave. The remaining display time refreshes after said condition is met again. Achievements Jolly Jamboree • “What you're celebrating doesn't matter, just throw a party already!” Throw a Party . NPCs are friendly automated non-player characters that provide services to players. [ 1 ] Standing near an NPC and pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on them will open a dialogue window, with the portrait of the NPC and one or more options for the player to select. Some NPCs are vendors , displaying a shop inve

ct. Some NPCs are vendors , displaying a shop inventory when the “ Shop ” option is selected. They sell items to players in exchange for coins , and can be sold items by a player to obtain coins. Some NPCs also have other options that perform other functions. The style of NPC portraits can be changed in settings . Most NPCs appear on the minimap as icons of their heads. Hovering the cursor on an icon displays the NPC's name and type. For example, a Guide named Andrew is shown on the minimap as A

a Guide named Andrew is shown on the minimap as Andrew the Guide . Most NPCs are town NPCs (also known as villagers [ 2 ] or townsfolk [ 3 ] ), appearing after various milestones are achieved through in-game advancement. They move into structures the player must provide, known as houses , which are generally required for town NPCs to remain available and respawn if killed. Each NPC will need their own house. NPCs can reduce enemy spawn rates in the area, the effectiveness of which will increase

the area, the effectiveness of which will increase as more NPCs are present, capped at 3 NPCs. [ 4 ] However, this enemy spawn reduction effect will be weaker in the Underworld , and is negated in some circumstances (see effect of friendly town NPCs on spawn rates for details). Players tend to use this effect by building large structures (e.g. bases ) with several integrated houses, to serve as their safe area. Town NPCs generally walk around during the day . They remain still in their assigned

ing the day . They remain still in their assigned houses at night and during certain events. NPCs have limited health and can take damage , and ultimately die from enemies , traps , lava , or by drowning in water ; however, they cannot be attacked directly by players (outside of special Voodoo Doll accessories, Rotten Eggs , or the Flymeal ). They do not suffer from fall damage and their health regenerates over time. Furthermore, the Nurse can heal herself and other nearby NPCs. Additionally: To

al herself and other nearby NPCs. Additionally: Town NPCs will attack nearby enemies, with each NPC having their own unique form of attack. They will not actively pursue combat, however, but rather attack in an act of self-defense. Town NPCs gain defense and damage boosts based on which bosses have been defeated in the current world. Occasionally, NPCs sit in chairs and randomly "talk" to other NPCs or the player, shown as mouth movement and speech bubbles known as emotes , which change based on

ch bubbles known as emotes , which change based on various conditions. If an NPC is killed, they will respawn after a delay, as long as a suitable vacant house is still available. There is a total of 26 town NPCs, 8 of whom normally appear in Hardmode . An additional 3 NPCs can be encountered that do not move into houses. Spawning NPCs that can live in houses will spawn after their specific spawning conditions were met and there is an empty valid house. Other NPCs will spawn either randomly or r

house. Other NPCs will spawn either randomly or regularly (see their respective page for details). After an NPC moves into a valid house, they will be permanently unlocked in the current world. If an unlocked NPC is killed, they will respawn as long as any empty valid house exists, without having to meet the spawning conditions again. Additionally: NPCs will not spawn during an active event (except the Lunar Events ) or at night (7:30 PM – 4:30 AM). An NPC takes about 2 minutes to spawn after a

AM). An NPC takes about 2 minutes to spawn after another NPC has spawned or dawn (4:30 AM) passes. If valid housing is off-screen during spawning, the NPC will spawn directly into the house . Rescuable NPCs Some NPCs and town pets have to be rescued before they can move into houses, with some of them being referred to as Bound NPCs due to their appearance. They will randomly spawn in their respective biomes and respawn if they despawned or be killed, just like how enemies and critters spawn. Nor

led, just like how enemies and critters spawn. Normally, pressing the ⚷ Open / Activate button on them will rescue and unlock them, but certain NPCs may have to be rescued in other ways. Once unlocked, they will be able to respawn after being killed (just like regular NPCs), without having to rescue them again. [ 6 ] The arrival of a homeless NPC is signaled by one of the following status messages : “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, searching for a place to rest. ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, looking for a home! ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived

e! ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, wishing for a house! ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, desiring shelter! ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, hoping to settle down! ” “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has arrived, looking to settle nearby. ” The homeless NPC will leave if they still do not have a house by nightfall (after 6:00 PM), displaying the status message “ <Name of NPC> the <Type of NPC> has departed! ” House  Two town NPCs cannot live in the

eparted! ” House  Two town NPCs cannot live in the same house at the same time (but town pets may share a house with non-town pet NPCs). Houses must not be located near Corruption or Crimson , and if those biomes spread too close to an occupied house, the NPC living there may move out from that house. Town NPC home assignments can be rearranged using NPC flags from the housing menu , where players can also check whether a room is a suitable house with the housing query . If both a town NPC and t

with the housing query . If both a town NPC and their house are offscreen, they will teleport to their house during night , rain , Solar Eclipses , or Slime Rain . The Old Man will consider the Dungeon point (the spawn point of the Old Man, i.e. the left of the two tiles on which he stands during night) as his "house", and he will always teleport to this location if he is offscreen and standing on Dungeon Bricks , regardless of time. [ 7 ] [ 8 ] If a house occupied by an NPC is damaged in a way

If a house occupied by an NPC is damaged in a way that makes it invalid, the NPC will move out and walk around the area until a suitable house is restored. If there are vacant houses available in the world, the NPC may move into one of them. If the NPC gets killed and does not have a home, they will not move back in until a new house becomes available. Vendor NPCs Some NPCs are Vendor NPCs that have a “ Shop ” option in the dialogue window. Selecting the option will display the NPC's shop inven

cting the option will display the NPC's shop inventory similar to the player's own inventory . Items can be bought by moving them from the shop inventory into the player's inventory; the cost in coins will be subtracted from the player's storage as soon as the item is picked up from the shop inventory. An item can only be bought if its coin cost is available in the player's inventory (or in the combined player's savings). Holding the ⚒ Use / Attack or ⚷ Open / Activate button on stackable items

ck or ⚷ Open / Activate button on stackable items allows the player to buy the item in bulk, with the buying speed increases the longer the button is held; holding the ⇧ Left Shift at the same time will buy the item in batches of 10. Some vendor NPCs sell biome-specific items when they are spoken to in certain biomes . They can be transported there by housing them there and waiting for the night (they will be teleported to their house at night once off-screen) or by using a King or Queen Statue

ce off-screen) or by using a King or Queen Statue . Note that the NPC does not need to live in the biome; talking to them while the player is in the biome is sufficient. All vendor NPCs can also be used to sell unwanted or surplus items to gain coins. The unwanted item(s) must be in the player's inventory. Hovering over the item while the NPC's inventory screen is open will display its sell value. Items are always sold to vendors at 1/5 of their purchase price, though items cannot be sold if the

purchase price, though items cannot be sold if the player has no space for the coins. Not all items have a sell value (very common items such as Dirt or Wood have no value). Placing the item in the NPC's shop inventory will sell it, granting the player the sell value into their inventory. Sales to (and purchases from) NPCs can be canceled while the NPC's inventory screen is still open: Sold items can be taken back in exchange for the amount they were sold for (and purchased items can be sold bac

were sold for (and purchased items can be sold back in exchange for the amount they were purchased for). All vendor NPCs have the same base buying and selling price. Item prices can be decreased by equipping a Discount Card , which is a rare drop from Pirate Invasions . Prices can also be higher or lower based on the NPC's happiness level . All vendor NPCs, with the exception of the Tavernkeep , Skeleton Merchant , and Traveling Merchant , will sell pylons when another NPC is nearby. Notes The T

ell pylons when another NPC is nearby. Notes The Truffle has special rules for houses he can move into, but his spawning only requires the world to be in Hardmode , so he can spawn as homeless NPC as soon as Hardmode starts. Vendor NPCs who have biome requirements to sell certain items need to be spoken to with the player being in said biome to fulfill the requirement. It is not sufficient to have their home located in the specific biome. Due to how damage calculations are performed, hostile pro

how damage calculations are performed, hostile projectiles deal reduced damage to NPCs. The Oasis mini-biome counts as a Desert in terms of an NPC's preferred biome. Similarly, a Graveyard counts as the host biome, and (dialogue aside) being in a Graveyard does not affect NPC happiness. NPCs can drown in water that is 2 tiles deep, whereas players require 3 tiles of water depth in order to drown. NPCs will not move and always face the player as long as their dialogue window is open. They also gain a damage multiplier based on the difficulty of the world: 1× / 1.5× / 1.75× , respectively. In a Journey Mode world, the damage multiplier is 1× when the diffi

world, the damage multiplier is 1× when the difficulty is lower than Expert (2×), and is set to 2× when the difficulty is no less than Expert (2×). The damage boost stacks multiplicatively with the difficulty damage multiplier. The attack speed bonus stacks multiplicatively with itself. When selecting “ Happiness ” in an NPC's dialogue window, the NPC will tell the player if any factors make them currently happy or unhappy, although they will not say what could make them happier or unhappier. For

say what could make them happier or unhappier. For example, if the Guide is located alone in a Forest , he will praise the lack of other NPCs around and the fact he is in a Forest, but he will not hint at the fact he would prefer to live near the Clothier or Zoologist . One can, however, find an NPC's preferred biome from the background of the portrait in their Bestiary entry, with the exception of the Princess , who has no biome preference but has a portrait background of The Hallow . Factors t

as a portrait background of The Hallow . Factors that make an NPC happy will lower their prices for goods purchased from them, down to a minimum of 3/4 (75%) , and increase the value of goods sold to them by the inverse of the same modifier, up to a maximum of 4/3 (133%) . Conversely, factors that make an NPC unhappy will raise their prices for goods purchased from them, up to a maximum of 3/2 (150%) , and decrease the value of goods sold to them, down to a minimum 2/3 (67%) . Price modifiers ar

, down to a minimum 2/3 (67%) . Price modifiers are rounded to the nearest 1% increment. The Tax Collector 's current tax funds and tax collection limit are both divided by his happiness factor, such that at maximum happiness he will store up to 33 GC 33 SC 33 CC , and at minimum happiness he will only store up to 16 GC 66 SC 66 CC ; the amount of taxes that he collects per minute also scales at the same rate. Icon Price factor ≤82% ≤100% ≤110% >110% For the purpose of calculating distances below, the position of an NPC's home tile is considered instead of the NPC itself, unless the NPC is homeless in which case their actual position is considered. The home tile is the block where NPCs stand on during nighttime and events. NPC's flag will "hook" directly above the home tile. One can easil

"hook" directly above the home tile. One can easily find the home tile by locating the central column of the 3×3-tile area that the NPC's flag occupies, and then moving all the way down to the tile in the floor of the house. For more detailed definition of a home tile, see House § Home tile score . Biome-related preferences will reference the primary biome where the player is, rather than where the NPC or their home is. If the player is in a hybrid biome , and the NPC loves/likes one of the biom

id biome , and the NPC loves/likes one of the biomes, but hates/dislikes one of the others, the loved/liked biome will take priority. Biomes that the NPC is neutral to are ignored. An NPC's happiness is computed the moment their chat window is opened, [ 17 ] which means that their happiness can be altered very quickly (e.g. by closing their chat window, adding or removing nearby NPC flags, and re-opening the chat window). Category: コレクター Tips Each time the Traveling Merchant spawns, he will have a random sho

aveling Merchant spawns, he will have a random shop inventory. If the player leaves the world between 4:30 AM and 12:00 PM, and enters again, there is a 22% chance he will re-spawn (with a new shop inventory). Bound NPCs found underground can be freed by talking to them, after which they will always respawn whenever a vacant house is available. Having an empty room ready for them beforehand will cause them to move in at night as soon as they are off-screen. Bound NPCs can be found by listening t

off-screen. Bound NPCs can be found by listening to their "hurt" sounds , especially the Mechanic , as the Dungeon is teeming with enemies. A housing area can be created where all the NPCs can have their biome-specific items: 1500 Pink Ice Blocks , 101 Mushroom grass , 140 Jungle grass / Lihzahrd Bricks , 1500 Sand Blocks , and 7 Gravestones at the Space layer near the houses are located, within the biome-checking area . Drowning an NPC in water is an easy way to get NPC drops (safer than lava a

is an easy way to get NPC drops (safer than lava and quicker than Rotten Eggs ). When the For the Worthy special seed is active, the player's tombstone may also be used to get NPC drops. To open a chest behind an NPC, press the ⚷ Open / Activate button twice. The first press will open the NPC's dialogue box, the second one will open the Chest. Trivia During a Blood Moon, all female NPCs except the Party Girl and Princess have dialogue that is more irritable or aggressive than normal. This refere

e irritable or aggressive than normal. This references premenstrual syndrome , which may cause mood swings and irritability in some people. Both the menstrual cycle and the real-world moon cycle occur approximately every 28 days. The only NPC interactions required to reach the defeat of the Moon Lord are talking to the Old Man to summon Skeletron and allow the Cultists to spawn, and killing the Guide with the Guide Voodoo Doll to fight the Wall of Flesh . Skeletons will not attack the Skeleton M

f Flesh . Skeletons will not attack the Skeleton Merchant , and vice versa. The Party Girl is the only NPC to "love" two NPCs as one of their living preference(s). The Skeleton Merchant and the Traveling Merchant originally had special price modifiers , depending on the moon phase and distance from the world's edges, respectively. These modifiers were deactivated quickly after their introduction, in 1.4.0.4 , but still remain in the code. The price modifier for the Skeleton Merchant would depend

ce modifier for the Skeleton Merchant would depend on the moon phase: [ 20 ] 110% during gibbous moons , 120% during quarter moons , 130% during crescent moons , and 140% during new moons . Additionally, his price modifier would be increased by another +10% during the day . The price modifier for the Traveling Merchant would range between 115% (at the world spawn point ) and 85% (at the world's borders). [ 21 ] The Guide and Old Man are unique in that they always spawn upon creating a world. The

that they always spawn upon creating a world. The Old Man does not move into a house, but rather remains near the Dungeon 's entrance, and respawns the following day if killed (while Skeletron has not yet been defeated). NPCs will emit bubble particles when underwater. The location of where the particles are emitted from is lower for the Angler (8 pixels lower than other NPCs), possibly due to him being a child. However, this does not apply to the Princess despite her also being a child. [ 22 ]

he Princess despite her also being a child. Desktop 1.4.5.3 : Added Profile, Retro, and Disabled styles for NPC portraits. The existing style is called "Detailed". Desktop 1.4.5.0 : Added portraits for all NPCs. Can now arrive even when there are no available houses. Only one will arrive per

are no available houses. Only one will arrive per day, and they will leave if they do not have a house by nightfall. NPCs now take priority over town pets when respawning. Homeless NPCs now have a Housing dialogue option instead of Happiness, containing instructions on how to build a suitable house . Can now attack enemies while talking to players. NPCs will now avoid players with the Stinky debuff. Defeating a boss now also gives each NPC a 1.5% attack speed bonus. Now also gain stat bonuses f

% attack speed bonus. Now also gain stat bonuses from defeating King Slime , Deerclops and Duke Fishron . Defeating Queen Slime , Duke Fishron and Empress of Light now boost the damage of Dryad's Ward by 15% each (same as other Hardmode bosses). Party Girl NPC count requirement to move in increased from 14 to 20, to account for town pets. Shops now have a visual indicator showing how NPC happiness is affecting prices. The Savings display in shops now caps the amount of Platinum Coins to 999, to

now caps the amount of Platinum Coins to 999, to prevent overflows. Fixed name-based special drops ( Green Cap , Ivy , and Jim's Cap ) not dropping when the game is set to certain languages. Desktop 1.4.4.4 : Fixed an issue where NPCs whose names were translated would not drop name-based special drops. This issue was not actually fixed until 1.4.5.0. gnoring the original unique conditions which were required to spawn in the first place. Desktop 1.4.3.3 : The minimum required happiness for NPCs to sell pylons has been decreased from 0.85 to 0.9. This means that it is now easier to make NPCs happy enough to sell a pylon. lowed Desert), and they like at least one of those biomes, then they will gain positive happiness from the biome they like. The maximum number of NPCs in a town before Crowded penalties begin to take effect has been increased from 4 to 5. However, 4-NPC towns will have neither a bonus nor a penalty. The maximum number of NPCs in a town before "hate crowded" quotes begin to appear has been increased from 7 to 8. Desktop 1.4.1.2 : Happiness price modifiers are now rounded to the nearest 1% increme

odifiers are now rounded to the nearest 1% increment instead of the nearest 5% increment. Reverted the change to the distances to compute happiness made in 1.4.1. NPC Happiness now gives the "Solitude" bonus for 3-NPC towns. Previously, there was neither a bonus nor a penalty at 3 NPCs. Desktop 1.4.1.1 : Fixed several grammatical/spelling issues in NPC dialogue. Fixed an issue where Town NPCs would try to sit on Dynasty Chairs (they should not be able to). Introduction Terraria world seeds allow for the near perfect recreation of world generation given the same seed and other relevant in-game settings. This means that if you get some funky world generation and want to share with friends or would simply like to restart your world anew for a different adventure, you can now do so! This guide will be a list of seeds that are abnormal in one way or another, whether it be interesting world generation, a lucky spawn location, easily accessible resources

lucky spawn location, easily accessible resources, a rare glitch, etc. Terraria updated seeds (Still working) The world seeds in the following section(s) are seeds that still work (generate the special features for which they are being highlighted) and are recreatable in current versions of Terraria. Infinite chest glitch money farm This world generates a glitched Living Wood Chest with a missing wood tile underneath, so when you try to destroy while it still has items inside, it drops four che

while it still has items inside, it drops four chests and the original stays in place. Farm them and sell them to any shop NPC for an easy infinite amount of money!
